$comment: FX alert rules as code (Phase 4, 2026-07-03). Source of truth in a clean schema; scripts/provision-grafana.js translates to the Grafana provisioning API at push time. PromQL mirrors grafana/dashboards/resplit-fx.json so panels and alerts cannot drift. Datasource is Grafana Cloud Prometheus (OTLP), NOT the retired GCP Managed Prometheus.

contactPoint.$comment: Per the plan Setup Contract: default email leojkwan@gmail.com unless overridden. Notification policy + Slack/PagerDuty routing live in Grafana, not here.

rules.title: FXSnapshotStale
rules.summary: FX snapshot dead-man's-switch: the daily publish stopped and clients are being served an ever-staler rate with no error.
rules.$why: 26h (93600s) gives a 2h grace past the daily publish cadence. This is the highest-value alert: a freshness dead-man is the only thing that catches a NON-erroring outage (the exact failure class this hardening responds to). noData => Alerting so a missing metric also pages (dead-man semantics).

rules.title: FXReadSLOFastBurn
rules.summary: The /quote read-path is burning its 99.9% error budget fast (5xx spike).
rules.$why: Multi-window fast-burn (5m AND 1h both > 14.4x the 0.1% budget = 0.0144 error ratio) per the Google SRE multi-burn-rate pattern — pages only on a real, sustained 5xx spike, not a single blip. noData => OK because zero traffic is not an error.

rules.title: FXFallbackShare
rules.summary: A sustained majority of /quote responses are non-exact (stale/derived) rates served as if fresh — silent staleness.
rules.$why: The top app trust risk: clients shown prior_day/today cross-rate fallbacks instead of the exact published rate. Pages when >25% of served quotes are non-exact for 30m (transient publish-lag blips self-heal well under that). noData => OK (no traffic != staleness).
